response:
the engineer models the query response time for each database as a function of the number of queries q it handles  
mysql: t_mysql(q) = a * q² + b * e^{c * q}  
oracle: t_oracle(q) = d * ln(q + 1) + e / (q + 1)  
sybase: t_sybase(q) = f * q^(3/2) - g * sqrt(q)  
total query response time function t_total(q) = t_mysql(α * q) + t_oracle(β * q) + t_sybase((1 - α - β) * q)  

the optimal values of α and β that minimize the total query response time t_total(q) are determined by finding the values that minimize the function t_total(q)  
the function t_total(q) is derived by substituting the expressions for t_mysql, t_oracle, and t_sybase into the equation for t_total(q)  
the optimal values of α and β are found by taking the partial derivatives of t_total(q) with respect to α and β and setting them to zero  
the partial derivatives are solved to find the values of α and β that minimize the total query response time  

the optimal values of α and β are found by solving the system of equations derived from setting the partial derivatives to zero  
the solution to the system of equations gives the optimal values of α and β that minimize the total query response time  
the optimal values of α and β are determined by solving the equations for the partial derivatives of t_total(q)  
the solution to the equations gives the optimal values of α and β that minimize the total query response time
